title: Veeshas Tuwan
content:

{{Quote|Perhaps the greatest loss during that period was Veeshas Tuwan, an ancient Sith library on the Sith world Arkania. For destroying our history, all Jedi deserve the most painful death Lord Kaan can conceive!|An extract from [[Seviss Vaa]]'s account of the [[post–Great Hyperspace War counterinvasion]] recorded in ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
'''Veeshas Tuwan''' was an extensive [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[library-temple]] buried in the frozen wastes of [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] that served the [[Sith Empire/Legends|old Sith Empire]] as a repository of [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] and [[Sith scroll/Legends|writings]]. The structure was destroyed by a strike team of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] during the joint [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]–[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] [[Post–Great Hyperspace War counterinvasion|counterinvasion]] that followed the conclusion of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].

==Description==
Veeshas Tuwan was a sprawling [[library-temple]] and record hall complex situated deep in the frozen wastes of [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]]. At its fullest extent, Veeshas Tuwan covered an area of more than five square [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] and extended deep underground for many levels. As one of the few record halls of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|old Sith Empire]], Veeshas Tuwan was home to an extensive collection of [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] and [[Sith scroll/Legends|writings]] drawn from across the Empire. So extensive was Veeshas Tuwan's collected contents that even the most experienced [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] faced difficulty in locating and retrieving the information they sought. Like many library-temples, the complex likewise included a number of sealed chambers in which [[Sith sorcerer]]s conducted [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-sided]] experiments and transcribed rituals of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]].<ref name="TotJC" />

==History==
The Veeshas Tuwan library-temple was constructed around [[7000 BBY]],<ref name="TEGtAS" /> around the time of the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]] and the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles]]' subsequent reorganization of the [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|ancient Sith Empire]] into the old Sith Empire.<ref name="JvS" /> Located on frigid [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]], a [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] world known for producing mad scientists and rogue geneticists since the [[Indecta Era]],<ref name="AA">''[[Alien Anthology]]''</ref><ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref> Veeshas Tuwan constituted one of the nascent Empire's earliest colonies located outside the veil of the [[Stygian Caldera]].<ref name="TEA" /> The complex came to serve as one of the Empire's primary record halls and repositories of Sith artifacts, lore, and writings over a span of millennia. At its fullest extent, the library was so extensive that even the most experienced Sith Lords faced difficulty locating and retrieving the information they sought.<ref name="TotJC" />

Veeshas Tuwan met its end during the joint [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]–[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] [[Post–Great Hyperspace War counterinvasion|counterinvasion]] that followed the conclusion of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]].<ref name="SWTORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref><ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Around the time of the Sith Empire's collapse, the Jedi Order became aware of Veeshas Tuwan's existence on Arkania. In response to the presence of a Sith colony so close to the heart of the Republic,<ref name="TEA" /> a Jedi strike force composed of hundreds of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]]<ref name="TotJC" /> and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]]<ref name="TCSWEIII299" /> was dispatched to Arkania and tasked with razing the Veeshas Tuwan complex to the ground. While most of the library's artifacts and scrolls were destroyed to prevent the Sith from returning to power at some indeterminate point in the future,<ref name="TotJC" /> a handful of significant writings were stolen and preserved by the Jedi for subsequent study.<ref name="TCSWEIII299" />

Subsequent generations of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], particularly the various iterations of [[New Sith]] who arose in the centuries following [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin's]] [[Fourth Great Schism]], commonly perceived the destruction of Veeshas Tuwan and the loss of its collated lore as one of the greatest blows ever dealt to the Sith religion in its entire history.<ref name="TCSWEIII299" /> Sith Lord [[Seviss Vaa]], a ranking member of [[Skere Kaan]]'s [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] and a scholar familiar with the history of the old Sith Empire, personally believed that the Jedi deserved the most painful deaths imaginable for their destruction of the Veeshas Tuwan, as this act robbed later generations of Sith of the greater part of their cultural heritage and history.<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Veeshas Tuwan was the subject of a minor [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] with the publication of its entry in 2008's ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. While 1996's ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' stated clearly that "[n]ot a single resource was saved, for the Jedi feared any remaining dark-side knowledge might allow the Sith to return to power at some point in the future," the subject's ''Encyclopedia'' entry implies that the Jedi retrieved at least some of the library's contents before razing it to the ground, as the relevant entry reads "a great portion of [Sith] history was destroyed or stolen by the Jedi."
